# **Educational Hardware Builder — Refined Blueprint v2**

## **Vision**

A first-principles learning engine for DIY hardware. Users don't just follow instructions — they understand *why* each component works, taught through Socratic micro-lessons woven into real builds. Long-term, the platform evolves from guided builds toward reverse-engineering and modifying everyday appliances and furniture.

**Product philosophy:** Because the platform teaches physical construction, *accuracy is the product*. Every architectural decision below biases toward ground-truth data and deterministic validation over generative flexibility.

---

## **Locked Architecture Decisions**

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Frontend | **Next.js 14+ (App Router) \+ React Three Fiber** | Deepest 3D ecosystem (drei, rapier); the 3D Mech View is the highest-risk component |
| LLM strategy | **Fully local via Ollama** | Privacy, zero inference cost, on-brand for DIY audience |
| Model runtime location | **User's own machine (consumer GPU, 8–16GB VRAM)** | Design target: quantized 8B–14B models |
| Orchestration | **Split: TypeScript code-first agents (real-time) \+ n8n (background jobs)** | Streaming/state/retries in code; n8n for scheduled ingestion |
| Databases | **PostgreSQL \+ pgvector** (single database) | One backup story; SQL joins between embeddings, parts, and inventory |
| Packaging | **Self-hosted docker-compose** | Zero hosting cost, full privacy, matches tinkerer audience |
| MVP vertical | **Electronics / microcontroller projects only** | Best structured data coverage (Octopart, GrabCAD, iFixit) |
| Generative 3D fallback | **Parametric OpenSCAD template library only** (raw codegen deferred to Phase 3\) | Small local models fill validated parameters; every output compiles deterministically |

---

## **1\. System Overview**

One `docker compose up` brings up the entire stack on the user's machine:

![][image1]

**Compose services:** `web` (Next.js), `agents` (Node worker, can be merged into `web` for MVP), `postgres` (pgvector image), `ollama` (with NVIDIA/ROCm GPU passthrough), `n8n`, `openscad` (headless CLI container used as a validation/compile service).

**Model pulls on first run:** an init script pulls the model set (below) and warms the embedding model.

---

## **2\. Frontend (Next.js \+ React Three Fiber)**

**Stack:** Next.js App Router, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, React Three Fiber \+ `@react-three/drei`, Zustand (client state), TanStack Query (server state), SSE for streaming agent output.

### **Tabs**

1. **Dashboard** — project prompt input, skill level indicator, Beginner/Intermediate toggle, resume-active-build card, GPU/model health widget (shows which models are loaded and VRAM headroom — important since inference is local).  
2. **Inventory (Import Parts)** — free-text and structured part entry. A small local model normalizes entries ("rasp pi 4" → canonical `raspberry-pi-4b`, linked to its CAD file and datasheet). Parts get canonical IDs so the Parts Agent can join against them in SQL.  
3. **Workshop (Active Lesson)** — split screen: left \= step list with embedded micro-lessons and Socratic checkpoints (step gating — the Next button is disabled until the checkpoint question is answered); right \= 3D Mech View synced to the current step.  
4. **3D Mech View** — R3F canvas. Features for MVP: orbit/pan/zoom (drei `OrbitControls`), per-step camera targets, exploded-view slider (interpolate each part along its assembly vector), part highlighting synced to the active instruction, STL/GLTF loading with draco compression, bounding-box display for debugging fit.  
5. **Gallery** — completed builds: BOM table, lesson transcript, canvas-snapshot thumbnail (`gl.domElement.toDataURL()` — no diffusion model needed for MVP, keeping VRAM free for the LLM).

   ### **Frontend guardrails**

* All agent output arrives as **typed JSON over SSE** (zod-validated) — never raw prose parsed by the client.  
* 3D transforms come from the database (computed server-side by the Assembly Agent \+ validators), never computed in the browser from LLM text.  
  ---

  ## **3\. Data Layer (PostgreSQL \+ pgvector)**

Single database, roughly these core tables:

* `users`, `projects`, `project_steps`, `lessons`, `checkpoint_answers`  
* `parts_catalog` — canonical parts: name, category, datasheet URL, price snapshot, CAD file reference, electrical specs (JSONB)  
* `user_inventory` — FK to `parts_catalog` where matched; free-form rows for scrap  
* `cad_assets` — STL/STEP files (stored on a docker volume, path \+ hash \+ license in DB)  
* `embeddings` — pgvector column \+ source metadata (iFixit guide, YouTube transcript chunk, datasheet section, patent excerpt)  
* `assembly_transforms` — per step, per part: position, quaternion, parent frame, mating features used

**Why this pays off:** retrieval queries can join semantics with structure, e.g. *"top-k assembly chunks similar to the query, filtered to parts present in this user's inventory"* — impossible with a detached vector store without denormalizing everything.

**Embedding model:** `nomic-embed-text` or `mxbai-embed-large` via Ollama (CPU-friendly, keeps GPU free for generation).

---

## **4\. Model Tiering (Consumer-GPU Budget)**

Target: 8–16GB VRAM, quantized (Q4/Q5) GGUF via Ollama.

| Role | Model (default) | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Router / classifiers / inventory normalization | Llama 3.2 3B | Fast, always loaded |
| Lesson Agent, Research synthesis, Parts reasoning | Llama 3.1 8B or Qwen 2.5 14B (if VRAM allows) | Main workhorse |
| Template parameter fill (3D fallback) | Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B | Structured JSON output, code-adjacent |
| Embeddings | nomic-embed-text | CPU |

**Hard rules for every agent call:**

1. `format: json` with an explicit JSON schema in the prompt; zod-validate the result; one automatic retry with the validation error injected; deterministic fallback after that.  
2. Low temperature (0.1–0.3) for anything structural; higher only for lesson prose.  
3. RAG context is mandatory for factual claims — agents cite source chunks, and the UI shows "source: iFixit guide \#x" links. With small models, retrieval carries the accuracy burden.  
   ---

   ## **5\. Agent Swarm (TypeScript, code-first)**

Runs in the Node backend as a typed pipeline (plain TS state machine for MVP; LangGraph.js optional if you want checkpointing later). All agents stream progress events to the UI over SSE.

**Pipeline:** `Router → Research → Build → Parts → (per-step loop: Lesson ↔ Assembly) → Gallery`

### **Research Agent — "The Scout"**

* Input: user prompt \+ skill level.  
* Retrieval: pgvector top-k across iFixit guides, YouTube transcript chunks, datasheets; patents deferred to Phase 3 (mechanical-logic diagrams matter more for the appliance-reverse-engineering era than for MVP electronics).  
* Output (JSON): project summary, candidate approaches ranked by safety and part availability, safety flags (mains voltage, LiPo handling, soldering).  
* **Safety gate:** projects involving mains AC are flagged and gated behind explicit warnings for Beginner mode.

  ### **Build Agent — "The Architect"**

* Decomposes the project into modules (Power, Compute, Sensing, Actuation, Structure) with declared interfaces between them (voltage rails, data buses, mounting points).  
* Output: a module graph — this becomes the skeleton for steps, lessons, and the exploded 3D view.

  ### **Parts Agent — "The Quartermaster"**

* SQL-joins module requirements against `user_inventory` first (dynamic inventory adaptation is a core teaching mechanic — the agent must justify substitutions: "your L298N can replace the DRV8833 here because...").  
* Missing parts → price/availability from the locally cached `parts_catalog` (refreshed nightly by n8n from Octopart; live Amazon/AliExpress calls deferred — affiliate API approval is slow and unnecessary for MVP).  
* Beginner mode → offers a curated kit (hand-assembled kit definitions in the DB for the MVP's \~10 seed projects).

  ### **Lesson Agent — "The Shop Teacher"**

* Generates micro-lessons per step, tone and depth switched by Beginner/Intermediate flag.  
* **Socratic checkpoints:** before flagged critical steps, emits a multiple-choice or short-answer conceptual question. The step is gated in the UI until answered; a wrong answer triggers a targeted re-explanation, not just "incorrect."  
* Records answers in `checkpoint_answers` → feeds the skill-level indicator on the Dashboard.

  ### **Assembly Agent — "The Spatial Drafter"**

This is where local-model weakness is most dangerous, so **the LLM never emits raw coordinates.** Instead:

1. Ground-truth CAD files carry **mating-feature metadata** (mounting hole positions, connector locations) — extracted once at ingestion time by a deterministic geometry script (Python \+ trimesh in the n8n pipeline), stored in `cad_assets`.  
2. The Assembly Agent's job is *symbolic*: choose which features mate ("motor holes A1–A4 → bracket holes B1–B4, fastener M3×8").  
3. A deterministic solver computes the actual transform matrix from the mated features and writes it to `assembly_transforms`.  
4. A validation pass checks for bounding-box intersections; failures bounce back to the agent with the collision report.

   ### **Gallery Agent — "The Documentarian"**

* Compiles BOM (with final prices), step list, lesson transcript, checkpoint results.  
* Thumbnail \= 3D canvas snapshot. Diffusion-model hero images deferred (VRAM contention with the LLM on consumer GPUs).  
  ---

  ## **6\. Hybrid 3D Engine Strategy (refined)**

**Tier 1 — Ground truth (always preferred):** Curated library of exact CAD for standardized parts (NEMA 17, Arduino/RPi boards, common sensors, servos, breadboards) pulled from GrabCAD/Thangs/GitHub via n8n, license-checked, normalized to binary STL \+ metadata. For MVP, hand-curate \~100 parts covering the seed projects rather than crawling broadly.

**Tier 2 — Parametric template library (the generative fallback):** \~20 hand-written OpenSCAD templates: L-bracket, flat adapter plate, standoff array, project enclosure (parametric box \+ lid), servo horn adapter, panel-mount bezel, cable clip, spacer/bushing, sensor mount, display frame. The LLM emits only a validated JSON parameter object (hole spacing, thickness, clearances — with per-template min/max bounds enforced in zod *before* compilation). The headless OpenSCAD container compiles to STL; compile failure or degenerate geometry (zero-volume mesh check via trimesh) auto-rejects.

**Tier 3 — Free-form OpenSCAD codegen:** deferred to Phase 3, behind an "experimental" flag, ideally with a larger optional model.

**Spatial mating:** deterministic feature-based solver as described in the Assembly Agent — LLM chooses *what* mates, math computes *how*.

---

## **7\. Background Ingestion (n8n)**

Scheduled workflows, all writing into Postgres:

* **Nightly:** Octopart price/stock refresh for `parts_catalog`.  
* **Weekly:** iFixit guide sync (their API is open and well-structured — the backbone of the RAG corpus); YouTube transcript ingestion for a curated channel list (transcripts chunked → embedded → pgvector); GrabCAD/GitHub CAD watchlist check.  
* **On-demand (triggered from the app):** ingest a datasheet PDF the user uploads for a part not in the catalog.

Each pipeline ends with the same normalize → chunk → embed → upsert stage.

---

## **8\. Educational Mechanics (unchanged intent, sharpened implementation)**

* **Dynamic inventory adaptation** — enforced at the Parts Agent level with mandatory substitution justifications shown to the user.  
* **Curated beginner kits** — DB-defined for the 10 seed projects; guaranteed-compatible, one-click BOM.  
* **Adaptive difficulty** — a single flag threaded through Lesson \+ Parts \+ Research prompts; Beginner adds tool-identification lessons and safety callouts; Intermediate adds "why this component and not that one" comparisons.  
* **Socratic step gating** — checkpoints block progression; answers feed a lightweight mastery score per concept (Ohm's law, PWM, pull-up resistors...) that raises granularity where the user is weak.  
  ---

  ## **9\. Build Phases**

  ### **Phase 0 — Foundations (2–3 weeks)**

* Docker-compose skeleton: Next.js, Postgres+pgvector, Ollama with GPU passthrough, n8n, headless OpenSCAD.  
* Schema migration set; Ollama model-pull init script; SSE streaming plumbing with zod-validated agent messages.  
* **Exit criterion:** `docker compose up` on a clean machine with an NVIDIA GPU yields a working chat round-trip against a local model.

  ### **Phase 1 — The Golden Path (6–8 weeks)**

* **One hand-tuned seed project** (e.g., ESP32 weather station) built end-to-end: Research → Build → Parts → Workshop with lessons, Socratic gating, and a working 3D Mech View using \~30 curated CAD parts \+ 5 parametric templates.  
* iFixit \+ curated-YouTube ingestion pipelines live; parts catalog seeded via Octopart.  
* Beginner mode only. Inventory tab functional with LLM normalization.  
* **Exit criterion:** a real beginner completes the weather station using only the app, and every 3D transform came from the deterministic solver (zero LLM-emitted coordinates).

  ### **Phase 2 — Breadth & Adaptation (6–8 weeks)**

* Expand to \~10 seed projects and \~100 curated CAD parts; full 20-template library.  
* Intermediate mode; dynamic inventory substitution; mastery scoring; Gallery tab; kit definitions.  
* Hardening: model-tier auto-selection based on detected VRAM; collision-validation loop; retry/fallback telemetry (local, opt-in) to find where small models fail.  
* **Exit criterion:** a user can propose a project *not* in the seed list within the electronics vertical and get a coherent, safe build plan.

  ### **Phase 3 — Frontier (ongoing)**

* Experimental free-form OpenSCAD codegen (flagged); optional larger-model support for high-VRAM users.  
* Patents API integration for mechanical-logic diagrams.  
* Begin the reverse-engineering vertical: iFixit teardowns as first-class project inputs ("modify my appliance").  
* Live commerce APIs (Amazon PA-API / AliExpress) once affiliate approvals land.  
  ---

  ## **10\. Key Risks & Mitigations**

| Risk | Mitigation |
| ----- | ----- |
| Small local models produce wrong spatial/electrical facts | LLM never emits coordinates (deterministic solver); RAG-with-citation mandatory; JSON schema validation \+ retry \+ deterministic fallback |
| User hardware too weak (no GPU / low VRAM) | Startup VRAM detection → model-tier downgrade with explicit quality warning; CPU embedding models |
| Docker-compose install friction narrows audience | Acceptable for MVP (tinkerer audience); one-line install script; document a hosted option as a later fork |
| CAD licensing (GrabCAD terms vary) | License field on every `cad_assets` row; curate only redistributable/CC parts for the shipped seed library |
| Safety liability (mains voltage, LiPo, soldering) | Safety classifier gate in Research Agent; hard blocks in Beginner mode; persistent safety disclaimers per step category |
| n8n workflows drift from schema | All n8n pipelines terminate in a single versioned upsert API endpoint owned by the app, not direct DB writes |

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
